description: When an exposed surface cools, liquid water may condense there in small drops called 'suilo'. Dew commonly appears overnight on plants, roofs, stone, and webs. Rain falls from clouds, while dew forms where it rests; 'theriko' (frost) is ice rather than liquid water.
articulatory_notes: Send unvoiced air through a narrow groove along the tongue for 's' and round the lips into high-back 'u'. Keep the vowels separate as the tongue moves forward and unrounds into stressed high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and finish with middle-height rounded 'o'.

semantic_domains.nature: water condensed onto exposed surfaces under local cooling
semantic_domains.physical: small liquid drops resting on a surface
